short_description: Irrigation support through micro-irrigation, watershed development with subsidy up to 90%.
detailed_description: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) was launched with the motto of 'Har Khet Ko Pani' to extend the coverage of irrigation and improve water use efficiency. The scheme focuses on creating sources for assured irrigation, improving on-farm water use efficiency through micro-irrigation (drip and sprinkler), and watershed development. PMKSY provides financial assistance to farmers for adopting efficient irrigation systems.
key_benefits: Subsidy of 55% to 90% on micro-irrigation systems
Support for farm ponds and water harvesting structures
other_conditions: Land ownership or lease documents required
Subsidy percentage varies by category (Small/Marginal farmers get higher subsidy)
State government implements through agriculture department

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Proof of land ownership or lease agreement
Cost estimate from approved vendor for irrigation equipment
SC/ST/Small farmer certificate for higher subsidy

application_steps_online: Register with farmer details and land information
Select type of irrigation system required
application_steps_offline: Visit District Agriculture Office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra
Fill form with land and irrigation requirement details
Attach land records, Aadhaar, and bank details
Upon approval, install system through approved vendor
Subsidy will be transferred after installation verification

submission_locations.address: District Agriculture Office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra
submission_locations.notes: Implementation varies by state, contact local agriculture office

last_date: Open throughout the year (subject to budget availability)

faq.question: What is the subsidy percentage?
faq.answer: General farmers get 55% subsidy, while small/marginal farmers and SC/ST get up to 90% subsidy on micro-irrigation systems.

faq.question: Can I get subsidy for farm pond?
faq.answer: Yes, PMKSY provides support for farm ponds, water harvesting structures, and micro-irrigation systems.

faq.question: Do I need to pay first and then get subsidy?
faq.answer: Yes, typically you need to install the system first, then subsidy is transferred after verification by agriculture department.

common_rejection_reasons: Already availed subsidy for same land
Land not suitable for proposed irrigation system
tips_for_successful_application: Apply early in the financial year when budget is available
Get land records updated and clear
Consult agriculture officer for suitable irrigation system
Keep all receipts and installation certificates
